Gnaphalium saxatile, also known as rock cudweed, is a perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) characterized by its small white flowers and narrow leaves.

The health benefits of Gnaphalium saxatile include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of allergies, digestive issues, and other inflammatory conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Gnaphalium saxatile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Gnaphalium saxatile include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
